examples.title: Stack Implementation using Array
examples.description: Complete stack implementation with push, pop, display and status checking
examples.explanation: Array-based stack with top pointer starting at -1. Push increments top and adds element. Pop returns element and decrements top. Overflow when top reaches MAXSIZE-1, underflow when top is -1.

examples.title: Circular Queue Implementation
examples.description: Efficient queue implementation that wraps around using modular arithmetic
examples.explanation: Uses modular arithmetic (%) to wrap rear and front around. Full condition uses (rear+1)%MAXSIZE == front to distinguish from empty state. Solves linear queue space wastage problem.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for both enqueue and dequeue

examples.title: Infix to Postfix Conversion Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to convert infix expression to postfix using operator precedence
examples.code:
FUNCTION infixToPostfix(infix)
 Stack s
 OUTPUT postfix = ""
 
 FOR each character c in infix
 IF c is operand
 postfix = postfix + c
 ELSE IF c is '('
 Push c to s
 ELSE IF c is ')'
 WHILE s.top != '('
 postfix = postfix + Pop(s)
 Pop(s) // Remove '('
 ELSE // c is operator
 WHILE s is not empty AND precedence(s.top) >= precedence(c)
 postfix = postfix + Pop(s)
 Push c to s
 END FOR
 
 WHILE s is not empty
 postfix = postfix + Pop(s)
 
 RETURN postfix
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Operators with higher precedence are pushed first. When ')' encountered, pop until '('. At end, pop all remaining operators. Result follows operand-order-then-operator pattern.

examples.title: Postfix Expression Evaluation
examples.description: Algorithm to evaluate postfix expression using stack
examples.code:
FUNCTION evaluatePostfix(postfix)
 Stack s
 
 FOR each character c in postfix
 IF c is operand
 Push value of c to s
 ELSE IF c is operator
 operand2 = Pop(s) // Second operand
 operand1 = Pop(s) // First operand
 result = Apply operator c on operand1, operand2
 Push result to s
 END FOR
 
 RETURN Pop(s)
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION Apply(op, a, b)
 IF op == '+' RETURN a + b
 IF op == '-' RETURN a - b
 IF op == '*' RETURN a * b
 IF op == '/' RETURN a / b
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Scan postfix left to right. When operand: push to stack. When operator: pop two operands, apply operator (first pop is second operand), push result. Final result is single value on stack.

examples.title: Parentheses Balancing using Stack
examples.description: Program to check if parentheses in expression are balanced
examples.explanation: Push all opening brackets to stack. When closing bracket encountered, pop and check if they match. At end, if stack is empty, parentheses are balanced. Returns 1 for balanced, 0 for unbalanced.
